name.description: The name of this alert channel

number.description: The mobile number to receive the alerts

address.description: The email address of this email alert channel.

channel.description: The name of the alert's Slack channel

name.description: The name of this alert channel

number.description: The mobile number to receive the alerts

webhook_type.description: Type of the webhook. Possible values are 'WEBHOOK_DISCORD', 'WEBHOOK_FIREHYDRANT', 'WEBHOOK_GITLAB_ALERT', 'WEBHOOK_SPIKESH', 'WEBHOOK_SPLUNK', 'WEBHOOK_MSTEAMS' and 'WEBHOOK_TELEGRAM'.

block.description: Allows you to define alerting channels for the checks and groups in your account

activated.description: Determines if the check is running or not. Possible values `true`, and `false`.

degraded_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds starting from which a check should be considered degraded. Possible values are between 0 and 30000. (Default `15000`).

double_check.description: Setting this to `true` will trigger a retry when a check fails from the failing region and another, randomly selected region before marking the check as failed. (Default `false`).

environment_variables.description: Key/value pairs of environment variables to insert into the runtime environment.

frequency.description: Controls how often the check should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `0` (high frequency - use `frequency_offset` to define the actual frequency), `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

frequency_offset.description: Only relevant when `type` is `API`. When `frequency` is `0` (high frequency), `frequency_offset` is required and it alone controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in seconds. The allowed values are `0` (disabled - use `frequency` to define the actual frequency), `10` (10 seconds), `20` (20 seconds) and `30` (30 seconds).

group_id.description: The id of the check group this check is part of.

group_order.description: The position of this check in a check group. It determines in what order checks are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

local_setup_script.description: A valid piece of Node.js code to run in the setup phase.

local_teardown_script.description: A valid piece of Node.js code to run in the teardown phase.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run the this check. (Default ["us-east-1"])

max_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds starting from which a check should be considered failing. Possible values are between 0 and 30000. (Default `30000`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when a check fails/degrades/recovers.

private_locations.description: An array of one or more private locations slugs.

run_parallel.description: Determines if the check should run in all selected locations in parallel or round-robin.

runtime_id.description: The id of the runtime to use for this check.

script.description: A valid piece of Node.js JavaScript code describing a browser interaction with the Puppeteer/Playwright framework or a reference to an external JavaScript file.

setup_snippet_id.description: An ID reference to a snippet to use in the setup phase of an API check.

should_fail.description: Allows to invert the behaviour of when a check is considered to fail. Allows for validating error status like 404.

ssl_check.description: Determines if the SSL certificate should be validated for expiry.

ssl_check_domain.description: A valid fully qualified domain name (FQDN) to check its SSL certificate.

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks.

teardown_snippet_id.description: An ID reference to a snippet to use in the teardown phase of an API check.

type.description: The type of the check. Possible values are `API`, `BROWSER`, and `MULTI_STEP`.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this check.

activated.description: Whether an alert should be sent to this channel.

channel_id.description: The ID of the alert channel.

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your check, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

escalation_type.description: Determines the type of escalation to use. Possible values are `RUN_BASED` and `TIME_BASED`. (Default `RUN_BASED`).

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the check is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive check runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

block.description: Configuration for run-based escalation.

enabled.description: No longer available.

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the check has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the check.

key.description: The name of the environment variable or secret.

locked.description: If true, the value is not shown by default, but it can be accessed. (Default `false`).

secret.description: If true, the value will never be visible. (Default `false`).

value.description: The value of the environment variable or secret.

block.description: Insert environment variables into the runtime environment. Only relevant for browser checks. Use global environment variables whenever possible.

body_type.description: The `Content-Type` header of the request. Possible values `NONE`, `JSON`, `FORM`, `RAW`, and `GRAPHQL`.

ip_family.description: IP Family to be used when executing the api check. The value can be either IPv4 or IPv6.

method.description: The HTTP method to use for this API check. Possible values are `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `HEAD`, `DELETE`, `PATCH`. (Default `GET`).

comparison.description: The type of comparison to be executed between expected and actual value of the assertion. Possible values `EQUALS`, `NOT_EQUALS`, `HAS_KEY`, `NOT_HAS_KEY`, `HAS_VALUE`, `NOT_HAS_VALUE`, `IS_EMPTY`, `NOT_EMPTY`, `GREATER_THAN`, `LESS_THAN`, `CONTAINS`, `NOT_CONTAINS`, `IS_NULL`, and `NOT_NULL`.

source.description: The source of the asserted value. Possible values `STATUS_CODE`, `JSON_BODY`, `HEADERS`, `TEXT_BODY`, and `RESPONSE_TIME`.

block.description: A request can have multiple assertions.
Set up HTTP basic authentication (username & password).
An API check might have one request config.

base_backoff_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before the first retry attempt. (Default `60`).

max_duration_seconds.description: The total amount of time to continue retrying the check/monitor (maximum 600 seconds). Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `600`).

max_retries.description: The maximum number of times to retry the check/monitor. Value must be between `1` and `10`. Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `2`).

same_region.description: Whether retries should be run in the same region as the initial check/monitor run. (Default `true`).

type.description: Determines which type of retry strategy to use. Possible values are `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, `EXPONENTIAL`, `SINGLE_RETRY`, and `NO_RETRIES`.

network_error.description: When `true`, retry only if the cause of the failure is a network error. (Default `false`).

block.description: Apply the retry strategy only if the defined conditions match.
A strategy for retrying failed check/monitor runs.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.

Check groups allow  you to group together a set of related checks, which can also share default settings for various attributes.

activated.description: Determines whether activated checks in the group should run or not. Deactivating the group will prevent all checks in the group from running, regardless of their activated state. (Default `true`).

concurrency.description: Determines the number of checks to run concurrently when triggering the check group via CI/CD or the API. (Default `1`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when a check in this group fails and/or recovers. Muting the group will deactivate all notifications for all checks in the group, regardless of their muted state. (Default `false`).

name.description: The name of the check group.

inline_script.description: A valid piece of Node.js code.

snippet_id.description: The ID of a code snippet. Code snippets are not available for new plans.

block.description: A script to run in the setup phase of an API check. Runs in addition to the check's own setup script.

inline_script.description: A valid piece of Node.js code.

snippet_id.description: The ID of a code snippet. Code snippets are not available for new plans.

block.description: A script to run in the teardown phase of an API check. Runs in addition to the check's own teardown script.

Check groups organize related checks together and allow optional shared configuration. 

Unlike `checkly_check_group` (v1), which always imposed implicit defaults for various settings, `checkly_check_group_v2` does not override any check settings by default. A group can be as minimal as just a name. Use the optional `enforce_*` blocks only when you explicitly want the group to override individual check settings.

The new `checkly_check_group_v2` resource should always be preferred over the old `checkly_check_group` resource.

certificate.description: The client certificate in PEM format.

host.description: The host domain that the certificate should be used for.

private_key.description: The private key for the certificate in PEM format.

trusted_ca.description: PEM formatted bundle of CA certificates that the client should trust. The bundle may contain many CA certificates.

block.description:
Use client certificates to authenticate your API checks to APIs that require mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication, or any other authentication scheme where the requester needs to provide a certificate.

Each client certificate is specific to a domain name, e.g. `acme.com` and will be used automatically by any API checks targeting that domain.

Changing the value of any attribute forces a new resource to be created.

checks_per_page.description: Determines how many checks to show per page. Possible values are between 1 and 20. (Default `15`).

custom_css.description: Custom CSS to be applied to the dashboard.

custom_domain.description: A custom user domain, e.g. 'status.example.com'. See the docs on updating your DNS and SSL usage.

custom_url.description: A subdomain name under 'checklyhq.com'. Needs to be unique across all users.

enable_incidents.description: Enable incident support for the dashboard. (Default `false`).

expand_checks.description: Expand or collapse checks on the dashboard. (Default `false`).

favicon.description: A URL pointing to an image file to use as browser favicon.

header.description: A piece of text displayed at the top of your dashboard.

hide_tags.description: Show or hide the tags on the dashboard. (Default `false`).

is_private.description: Set your dashboard as private and generate key.

key.description: The access key when the dashboard is private.

link.description: A link to for the dashboard logo.

logo.description: A URL pointing to an image file to use for the dashboard logo.

paginate.description: Determines if pagination is on or off. (Default `true`).

pagination_rate.description: How often to trigger pagination in seconds. Possible values `30`, `60` and `300`. (Default `60`).

refresh_rate.description: How often to refresh the dashboard in seconds. Possible values `60`, '300' and `600`. (Default `60`).

show_check_run_links.description: Show or hide check run links on the dashboard. (Default `false`).

show_header.description: Show or hide header and description on the dashboard. (Default `true`).

show_p95.description: Show or hide the P95 stats on the dashboard. (Default `true`).

show_p99.description: Show or hide the P99 stats on the dashboard. (Default `true`).

tags.description: A list of one or more tags that filter which checks to display on the dashboard.

use_tags_and_operator.description: Set when to use AND operator for fetching dashboard tags. (Default `false`).

width.description: Determines whether to use the full screen or focus in the center. Possible values are `FULL` and `960PX`. (Default `FULL`).

activated.description: Determines whether the monitor will run periodically or not after being deployed.

degraded_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds where the monitor should be considered degraded. Possible values are between `0` and `5000`. (Default `500`).

frequency.description: Controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `0` (high frequency - use `frequency_offset` to define the actual frequency), `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

frequency_offset.description: When `frequency` is `0` (high frequency), `frequency_offset` is required and it alone controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in seconds. The allowed values are `0` (disabled - use `frequency` to define the actual frequency), `10` (10 seconds), `20` (20 seconds) and `30` (30 seconds).

group_id.description: The ID of the check group that this monitor is part of.

group_order.description: The position of the monitor in the check group. It determines in what order checks and monitors are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run the this monitor.

max_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds where the monitor should be considered failing. Possible values are between `0` and `5000`. (Default `1000`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when the monitor fails and/or recovers. (Default `false`).

run_parallel.description: Determines whether the monitor should run on all selected locations in parallel or round-robin. (Default `false`).

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks and monitors.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this check.

activated.description: Whether an alert should be sent to this channel.

channel_id.description: The ID of the alert channel.

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your monitor, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

escalation_type.description: Determines the type of escalation to use. Possible values are `RUN_BASED` and `TIME_BASED`. (Default `RUN_BASED`).

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the monitor is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive monitor runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

block.description: Configuration for run-based escalation.

enabled.description: No longer available.

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the monitor has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the monitor.

grace.description: How long Checkly should wait before triggering any alerts when a ping does not arrive within the set period.

period.description: How often you expect a ping to the ping URL.

ping_token.description: Custom token to generate your ping URL. Checkly will expect a ping to `https://ping.checklyhq.com/[PING_TOKEN]`.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.

The `checkly_heartbeat` resource has been renamed to `checkly_heartbeat_monitor` to better reflect its position in the Checkly product lineup.

The old resource type will not be deprecated until the Checkly provider is updated to the Terraform Plugin Framework, which makes it possible to easily move resources between resource types.

We recommend using the `checkly_heartbeat_monitor` resource type for any new resources.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.
Heartbeats allows you to monitor your cron jobs and set up alerting, so you get a notification when things break or slow down.

activated.description: Determines whether the monitor will run periodically or not after being deployed.

degraded_packet_loss_threshold.description: The packet loss percentage where the monitor should be considered degraded. Possible values are between `0` and `100`. (Default `10`).

frequency.description: Controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `0` (high frequency - use `frequency_offset` to define the actual frequency), `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

frequency_offset.description: When `frequency` is `0` (high frequency), `frequency_offset` is required and it alone controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in seconds. The allowed values are `0` (disabled - use `frequency` to define the actual frequency), `10` (10 seconds), `20` (20 seconds) and `30` (30 seconds).

group_id.description: The ID of the check group that this monitor is part of.

group_order.description: The position of the monitor in the check group. It determines in what order checks and monitors are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run this monitor.

max_packet_loss_threshold.description: The packet loss percentage where the monitor should be considered failing. Possible values are between `0` and `100`. (Default `20`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when the monitor fails and/or recovers. (Default `false`).

run_parallel.description: Determines whether the monitor should run on all selected locations in parallel or round-robin. (Default `false`).

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks and monitors.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this monitor. (Default `true`).

activated.description: Whether an alert should be sent to this channel.

channel_id.description: The ID of the alert channel.

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your monitor, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

escalation_type.description: Determines the type of escalation to use. Possible values are `RUN_BASED` and `TIME_BASED`. (Default `RUN_BASED`).

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the monitor is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive monitor runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

block.description: Configuration for run-based escalation.

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the monitor has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the monitor.

ip_family.description: The IP family to use. Possible values are `IPv4` and `IPv6`. (Default `IPv4`).

ping_count.description: The number of ping packets to send. (Default `10`).

comparison.description: The type of comparison to be executed between expected and actual value of the assertion. Possible values are `EQUALS`, `NOT_EQUALS`, `GREATER_THAN` and `LESS_THAN`.

property.description: The property of the source to assert. For `LATENCY` source, possible values are `avg`, `min`, `max` and `stdDev`.

source.description: The source of the asserted value. Possible values are `LATENCY` and `JSON_RESPONSE`.

target.description: The target value for the assertion.

block.description: Assertions to validate the ICMP response.
The parameters of the ICMP request.

base_backoff_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before the first retry attempt. (Default `60`).

max_duration_seconds.description: The total amount of time to continue retrying the check/monitor (maximum 600 seconds). Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `600`).

max_retries.description: The maximum number of times to retry the check/monitor. Value must be between `1` and `10`. Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `2`).

same_region.description: Whether retries should be run in the same region as the initial check/monitor run. (Default `true`).

type.description: Determines which type of retry strategy to use. Possible values are `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, `EXPONENTIAL`, `SINGLE_RETRY`, and `NO_RETRIES`.

block.description: Apply the retry strategy only if the defined conditions match.
A strategy for retrying failed check/monitor runs.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

Creates an ICMP Monitor to check host availability using ping.

ends_at.description: The end date of the maintenance window.

repeat_ends_at.description: The date on which the maintenance window should stop repeating.

repeat_interval.description: The repeat interval of the maintenance window from the first occurrence.

repeat_unit.description: The repeat cadence for the maintenance window. Possible values `DAY`, `WEEK` and `MONTH`.

starts_at.description: The start date of the maintenance window.

tags.description: The names of the checks and groups maintenance window should apply to.

activated.description: Determines whether the check will run periodically or not after being deployed.

frequency.description: Controls how often the check should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

group_id.description: The ID of the check group that this check is part of.

group_order.description: The position of the check in the check group. It determines in what order checks and monitors are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run the this check.

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when the check fails and/or recovers. (Default `false`).

private_locations.description: An array of one or more private locations slugs.

run_parallel.description: Determines whether the check should run on all selected locations in parallel or round-robin. (Default `false`).

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks and monitors.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this check. (Default `true`).

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your check, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the check is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive check runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the check has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the check.

id.description: The ID of the code bundle.

metadata.description: The generated metadata of the code bundle.

block.description: Attaches a code bundle to the check.

key.description: The name of the environment variable or secret.

locked.description: If true, the value is not shown by default, but it can be accessed. (Default `false`).

secret.description: If true, the value will never be visible. (Default `false`).

value.description: The value of the environment variable or secret.

block.description: Insert environment variables into the execution environment.

auto_detect.description: Whether to automatically detect appropriate runtime environment configuration from the bundle. (Default `true`).

working_dir.description: The working directory in which runtime commands are executed. This is useful for monorepos or workspaces where the Playwright project is in a subdirectory. Use "." to explicitly specify the root.

version.description: The Playwright version to use. Defaults to the version detected from the code bundle's lockfile.

block.description: The list of devices that should be made available for Playwright. Defaults to chromium, firefox, and webkit.
Configure the Playwright capabilities that should be made available to the runtime environment.

command.description: The command used to install dependencies prior to running Playwright. The default value is the appropriate install command for your package manager (e.g. `npm install` for `npm`).

block.description: Customize the install step, which is used to initialize the environment prior to starting the test run.

command.description: The command used to run Playwright. The default value is the appropriate exec command for your package manager (e.g. `npx playwright test` for `npm`).

block.description: Customize the test step.
Customize the actions taken during test execution.
Configure the runtime environment of the Playwright check.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.
Creates a Playwright check from a code bundle.

metadata.description: An opaque blob of generated metadata. The value is not intended to be user-consumable and should be passed as-is to a Playwright check resource.

block.description: A prebuilt archive containing the code bundle.
A managed code bundle which can be used in Playwright Check Suite resources.

icon.description: Icon assigned to the private location.

script.description: Your Node.js code that interacts with the API check lifecycle, or functions as a partial for browser checks.

custom_domain.description: A custom user domain, e.g. "status.example.com". See the docs on updating your DNS and SSL usage.

favicon.description: A URL to an image file to use as the favicon of the status page.

logo.description: A URL to an image file to use as the logo for the status page.

name.description: The name of the status page.

redirect_to.description: The URL the user should be redirected to when clicking the logo.

url.description: The URL of the status page.

block.description: A list of services to attach to the card.
A list of cards to include on the status page.
Checkly status pages allow you to easily communicate the uptime and health of your applications and services to your customers.
Status page services represent functional pieces of your application or website, such as landing page, API, support portal etc.

activated.description: Determines if the check is running or not. Possible values `true`, and `false`.

degraded_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds starting from which a check should be considered degraded. Possible values are between 0 and 5000. (Default `4000`).

frequency.description: Controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `0` (high frequency - use `frequency_offset` to define the actual frequency), `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

frequency_offset.description: When `frequency` is `0` (high frequency), `frequency_offset` is required and it alone controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in seconds. The allowed values are `0` (disabled - use `frequency` to define the actual frequency), `10` (10 seconds), `20` (20 seconds) and `30` (30 seconds).

group_id.description: The id of the check group this check is part of.

group_order.description: The position of this check in a check group. It determines in what order checks are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run the this check. (Default ["us-east-1"])

max_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds starting from which a check should be considered failing. Possible values are between 0 and 5000. (Default `5000`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when a check fails/degrades/recovers.

private_locations.description: An array of one or more private locations slugs.

run_parallel.description: Determines if the check should run in all selected locations in parallel or round-robin.

runtime_id.description: The ID of the runtime to use for this check.

should_fail.description: Allows to invert the behaviour of when a check is considered to fail.

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this check.

channel_id.description: The ID of the alert channel.

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your monitor, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the monitor is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive monitor runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

block.description: Configuration for run-based escalation.

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the monitor has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the monitor.

data.description: The data to send to the target host.

hostname.description: The hostname or IP to connect to. Do not include a scheme or a port in this value.

ip_family.description: The IP family to use when executing the TCP check. The value can be either `IPv4` or `IPv6`.

port.description: The port number to connect to.

comparison.description: The type of comparison to be executed between expected and actual value of the assertion. Possible values are `EQUALS`, `NOT_EQUALS`, `HAS_KEY`, `NOT_HAS_KEY`, `HAS_VALUE`, `NOT_HAS_VALUE`, `IS_EMPTY`, `NOT_EMPTY`, `GREATER_THAN`, `LESS_THAN`, `CONTAINS`, `NOT_CONTAINS`, `IS_NULL`, and `NOT_NULL`.

source.description: The source of the asserted value. Possible values are `RESPONSE_DATA` and `RESPONSE_TIME`.

block.description: A request can have multiple assertions.
The parameters for the TCP connection.

base_backoff_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before the first retry attempt. (Default `60`).

max_duration_seconds.description: The total amount of time to continue retrying the check/monitor (maximum 600 seconds). Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `600`).

max_retries.description: The maximum number of times to retry the check/monitor. Value must be between `1` and `10`. Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `2`).

same_region.description: Whether retries should be run in the same region as the initial check/monitor run. (Default `true`).

type.description: Determines which type of retry strategy to use. Possible values are `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, `EXPONENTIAL`, `SINGLE_RETRY`, and `NO_RETRIES`.

block.description: Apply the retry strategy only if the defined conditions match.
A strategy for retrying failed check/monitor runs.

The `checkly_tcp_check` resource has been renamed to `checkly_tcp_monitor` to better reflect its position in the Checkly product lineup.

The old resource type will not be deprecated until the Checkly provider is updated to the Terraform Plugin Framework, which makes it possible to easily move resources between resource types.

We recommend using the `checkly_tcp_monitor` resource type for any new resources.

block.description: A request can have multiple assertions.
The parameters for the TCP connection.

base_backoff_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before the first retry attempt. (Default `60`).

max_duration_seconds.description: The total amount of time to continue retrying the check/monitor (maximum 600 seconds). Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `600`).

max_retries.description: The maximum number of times to retry the check/monitor. Value must be between `1` and `10`. Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `2`).

same_region.description: Whether retries should be run in the same region as the initial check/monitor run. (Default `true`).

type.description: Determines which type of retry strategy to use. Possible values are `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, `EXPONENTIAL`, `SINGLE_RETRY`, and `NO_RETRIES`.

block.description: Apply the retry strategy only if the defined conditions match.
A strategy for retrying failed check/monitor runs.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.
TCP checks allow you to monitor remote endpoints at a lower level.

check_id.description: The id of the check that you want to attach the trigger to.

token.description: The token value created to trigger the check

url.description: The request URL to trigger the check run.

group_id.description: The id of the group that you want to attach the trigger to.

token.description: The token value created to trigger the group

url.description: The request URL to trigger the group run.

activated.description: Determines whether the monitor will run periodically or not after being deployed.

degraded_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds where the monitor should be considered degraded. Possible values are between `0` and `30000`. (Default `3000`).

frequency.description: Controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in minutes. The allowed values are `0` (high frequency - use `frequency_offset` to define the actual frequency), `1` (1 minute), `2` (2 minutes), `5` (5 minutes), `10` (10 minutes), `15` (15 minutes), `30` (30 minutes), `60` (1 hour), `120` (2 hours), `180` (3 hours), `360` (6 hours), `720` (12 hours) and `1440` (24 hours).

frequency_offset.description: When `frequency` is `0` (high frequency), `frequency_offset` is required and it alone controls how often the monitor should run. Defined in seconds. The allowed values are `0` (disabled - use `frequency` to define the actual frequency), `10` (10 seconds), `20` (20 seconds) and `30` (30 seconds).

group_id.description: The ID of the check group that this monitor is part of.

group_order.description: The position of the monitor in the check group. It determines in what order checks and monitors are run when a group is triggered from the API or from CI/CD.

locations.description: An array of one or more data center locations where to run the this monitor.

max_response_time.description: The response time in milliseconds where the monitor should be considered failing. Possible values are between `0` and `30000`. (Default `5000`).

muted.description: Determines if any notifications will be sent out when the monitor fails and/or recovers. (Default `false`).

private_locations.description: An array of one or more private locations slugs.

run_parallel.description: Determines whether the monitor should run on all selected locations in parallel or round-robin. (Default `false`).

should_fail.description: Allows to invert the behaviour of when the monitor is considered to fail. (Default `false`).

tags.description: A list of tags for organizing and filtering checks and monitors.

use_global_alert_settings.description: When true, the account level alert settings will be used, not the alert setting defined on this monitor. (Default `true`).

activated.description: Whether an alert should be sent to this channel.

channel_id.description: The ID of the alert channel.

block.description: An array of channel IDs and whether they're activated or not. If you don't set at least one alert channel subscription for your monitor, we won't be able to alert you even if it starts failing.

escalation_type.description: Determines the type of escalation to use. Possible values are `RUN_BASED` and `TIME_BASED`. (Default `RUN_BASED`).

enabled.description: Whether parallel run failure threshold is enabled. Only applies if the monitor is scheduled for multiple locations in parallel. (Default `false`).

percentage.description: Percentage of runs that must fail to trigger alert. Possible values are `10`, `20`, `30`, `40`, `50`, `60`, `70`, `80`, `90`, and `100`. (Default `10`).

block.description: Configuration for parallel run failure threshold.

amount.description: Number of reminder notifications to send. Possible values are `0`, `1`, `2`, `3`, `4`, `5`, and `100000` (`0` to disable, `100000` for unlimited). (Default `0`).

interval.description: Interval between reminder notifications in minutes. Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Defines how often to send reminder notifications after initial alert.

failed_run_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the given number of consecutive monitor runs have failed. Possible values are between `1` and `5`. (Default `1`).

block.description: Configuration for run-based escalation.

minutes_failing_threshold.description: Send an alert notification after the monitor has been failing for the given amount of time (in minutes). Possible values are `5`, `10`, `15`, and `30`. (Default `5`).

block.description: Configuration for time-based escalation.
Determines the alert escalation policy for the monitor.

follow_redirects.description: Whether to follow HTTP redirects automatically. (Default `true`).

ip_family.description: IP family version to use for the connection. The value can be either `IPv4` or `IPv6`. (Default `IPv4`).

skip_ssl.description: Whether to skip SSL certificate verification. (Default `false`).

url.description: The URL to monitor. Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL.

comparison.description: The type of comparison to be executed between expected and actual value of the assertion. Possible values are `EQUALS`, `NOT_EQUALS`, `GREATER_THAN` and `LESS_THAN`.

source.description: The source of the asserted value. The only allowed value is `STATUS_CODE`.

target.description: The target value. Typically `200` when the source is `STATUS_CODE`.

block.description: Assertions to validate the HTTP response. URL monitors only support status code assertions.
The parameters of the HTTP request.

base_backoff_seconds.description: The number of seconds to wait before the first retry attempt. (Default `60`).

max_duration_seconds.description: The total amount of time to continue retrying the check/monitor (maximum 600 seconds). Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `600`).

max_retries.description: The maximum number of times to retry the check/monitor. Value must be between `1` and `10`. Available when `type` is `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, or `EXPONENTIAL`. (Default `2`).

same_region.description: Whether retries should be run in the same region as the initial check/monitor run. (Default `true`).

type.description: Determines which type of retry strategy to use. Possible values are `FIXED`, `LINEAR`, `EXPONENTIAL`, `SINGLE_RETRY`, and `NO_RETRIES`.

network_error.description: When `true`, retry only if the cause of the failure is a network error. (Default `false`).

block.description: Apply the retry strategy only if the defined conditions match.
A strategy for retrying failed check/monitor runs.

description.description: A detailed description of the incident.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether to notify subscribers when the incident is triggered.

service_id.description: The status page service that this incident will be associated with.

severity.description: The severity level of the incident. Possible values are `MINOR`, `MEDIUM`, `MAJOR`, and `CRITICAL`.

block.description: Create and resolve an incident based on the alert configuration. Useful for status page automation.
Creates a URL Monitor to check HTTP endpoint availability and response times.

addresses.description: Static IP addresses for Checkly's runner infrastructure.

id.description: ID of the static IPs data source.

ip_family.description: Specify the IP families you want to get static IPs for. Only `IPv6` or `IPv4` are valid options.

locations.description: Specify the locations you want to get static IPs for.
